page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9i.6xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each of the 24 vCPUs maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating hyperthreading resource sharing.

The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps, facilitating steady data transfer rates for network-dependent operations.

From a resource-density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable compute performance. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, including medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9i.6xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, the instance utilizes an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz with a single thread per core configuration. This server does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation and high memory capacity make this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that require predictable compute performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and enterprise application workloads.
